mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. A sustained monthly decline in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over the preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% in September 2010 suggests labor market slack remains elevated
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and economic momentum, which drives corporate earnings growth expectations and produces positive equity returns. An acceleration in month-over-month RSAFS growth above 0.5% triggers positive 20-day forward returns as markets reprice growth upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 367,670 million dollars in September 2010
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS growth and forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) combined with stable or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as low real rates support equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPIAUCSL at 218.372 in September 2010
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below its 3-month moving average, signaling weakening supply-side momentum and potential demand destruction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate earnings downgrades from capacity underutilization.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below its 3-month moving average by more than 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector health. When both series show month-over-month growth above their respective 6-month medians simultaneously, this interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day SPX returns as broad-based demand strength supports equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both exceed their respective 6-month median month-over-month growth rates in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 598.0 thousand units and RSAFS at 367,670 million dollars in August 2010
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal explains less than 3% of forward return variance over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 150,000 jobs per month on a 3-month moving average basis, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as sustained employment gains support consumption and corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month moving average monthly change exceeds 150,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,201 thousand persons in September 2010
regime_hint: labor market recovery with accelerating job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, creating a negative real rate environment. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 5-day SPX returns as markets anticipate eventual monetary tightening and real return compression on equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.372 and FEDFUNDS at 0.19% in September 2010
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns when real rates turn negative.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarter-over-quarter annualized basis falls below 2.0%, signaling economic deceleration. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as sub-trend growth reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and equity risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,194.9 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q2 2010
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations when GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
